original: Anastasius I begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Pope Urban II preaches to the West to wrest control of the Holy Land from the Seljuk Turks at the Council of Clermont, triggering the First Crusade
Battle of Cortenuova: Holy Roman Emperor Frederick II defeats the Second Lombard League
Battle of Westrozebeke/Roosebeke: French army defeats the Flemish army. Flemish leader Philip Van Artevelde killed and his corpse displayed
Selimiye Mosque, a masterpiece of Ottoman architecture, designed by imperial architect Mimar Sinan, officially opens in Edirne, Turkey
Dutch county of Groningen flooded by failure of dyke
Rabbi Shneur Zalman, author (Tanya), released from St Petersburg jail
Berners Street hoax: Theodore Hook bets he can make any address the most talked-about in London, proceeds to win by bringing London to a standstill
Adoption of the Constitution of the Kingdom of Poland
City of Kraków (Poland) declared a free republic state by the Congress of Vienna
American Statistical Association organizes in Boston
Opera "Bohemian Girl" by Michael William Balfe with a libretto by Alfred Bunn first produced in London
-29] Battle at Fort Esperanza, Texas
Battle of Payne's Farm, Virginia
2nd day of Battles at Waynesboro, Georgia
The New York Times dubs baseball "The National Game"
1st photograph of a meteor taken by Austro-Hungarian photographer Ladislaus Weinek in Prague, Austrian-Hungarian Empire
1st permit issued to drive a car through Central Park (Curtis P Brady)
Hermann Sudermann's "Ehre" premieres in Berlin
1st signal box for SF Police Department goes into operation
Prince Ito of Japan comes to St Petersburg hoping to get the Russians to grant Japan concessions in Korea, but later drops this goal and decides to make an alliance with Britain
U.S. Army War College is established in Washington, D.C.
NY's Penn Station opens as world's largest railway terminal
Audience throws vegetables at actors for 1st recorded time in US
Albanian National Flag adopted
Spanish protectorate in Morocco forms
1st British woman elected political agent (Grantham, Lincolnshire)

Treaty of Neuilly-sur-Seine signed between Bulgaria and the Allied Powers requiring Bulgaria to cede territory to Greece and the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes and cutting the country's access to the Aegean Sea
57,000 watch a High School football game (LA & Polytechnic tie 7-7)
In New York City, the first Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade is held
German Parliament ratifies treaty of Locarno
110,000 watch US Army & Navy play a 21-all tie
Italian & Albania sign peace treaty
KXL-AM in Portland OR begins radio transmissions
Restoration of Williamsburg, Virginia, begins
Poland & USSR sign non-aggression treaty
British 13th Army Corps reaches Tobruk in Libya
USSR begins a counter offensive, causes Germany to retreat
French navy at Toulon scuttles ships & subs so Nazis cannot seize them
4,000 shells detonate in RAF arms depot at Fauld, near Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire; village of Hanbury destroyed, at least 70 people killed
US 121st Infantry regiment opens assault on Hurtgen in Germany
General George Marshall named special US envoy to China
Trial against Dutch Fascist NSB leader Anton Mussert begins (later convicted and executed by firing squad)
English soccer team beats Netherlands, 8-2
Joe DiMaggiowins his 3rd MVP, beating Ted Williams by 1 vote
Honda 1st opens in America
Red Sox sign shortstop Lou Boudreau as a player to 2-year contract
Trial against RC clergy "imperialistic conspiracy" begins in Prague
1st rocket to intercept an airplane at White Sands, New Mexico
Cease-fire & demarcation zone accord signed in Panmunjon, Korea
KTBC TV channel 7 in Austin, TX (CBS) begins broadcasting
Only win ever for NFL's Dallas Texans (1-11), beating the Chicago Bears, 27-23 at the Rubber Bowl in Akron, Ohio
Indians 3rd baseman Al Rosen is unanimously named AL's MVP
Alger Hiss is released from prison after serving 44 months for perjury
1957 NFL Draft:Paul Hornungfrom University of Notre Dame first pick by Green Bay Packers
Elżbieta Krzesińska of Poland equals world record with leap of 6.53m to win the women's long jump gold medal at the Melbourne Olympics
Frances Goodrich and Albert Hackett's "The Diary of Anne Frank" premieres in Netherlands
USSR abrogates Allied war-time agreements on control of Germany
CBS radio cancels "Have Gun Will Travel"
Trailing 38-7 late in 3rd quarter, Buffalo Bills tie Broncos at 38-38
Gordie Howebecomes the first to play in 1,000 NHL games
KHAW TV channel 11 in Hilo, HI (NBC) begins broadcasting
1st Boeing 727 jet rolls out of assembly plant in Renton, Washington
Sumner Arthur Long's play "Never Too Late" premieres in NYC
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
The Convention on the Unification of Certain Points of Substantive Law on Patents for Invention is signed at Strasbourg
15-25,000 demonstrate against war in Vietnam in Washington, D.C.
1966 NFL Draft: University of Texas linebacker Tommy Nobis #1 pick by Atlanta Falcons
In highest-scoring NFL game, Washington Redskins defeat NY Giants 72-41
Uruguay adopts constitution
Gold pool nations pledge support of $35 per ounce gold price
The Beatles release their album "Magical Mystery Tour" in the US; issued as a double EP in the UK in December
Carl Morton (18-11 for last-place Expos), receives NL Rookie of Year
Test Cricket debut of Rodney "Iron Gloves" Marsh v England, Brisbane
Soviet Mars 2 becomes 1st spacecraft to crash land on Mars
Two Customs officials are shot by an Irish Republican Army sniper firing upon a British Army patrol investigating a bomb attack on a Customs Post near Newry, County Armagh
Pierre Trudeau forms Canadian government
Yanks trade Ellis, Torres & Spikes to Indians for Nettles & Moses
Gary Matthews wins NL Rookie of Year
MLB St. Louis Cardinals outfielder Bake McBride wins NL Rookie of Year

The Provisional IRA assassinates Ross McWhirter, after a press conference in which McWhirter announced a reward for the capture of those responsible for multiple bombings and shootings across England.
1st day-night one-day cricket international, Australia v WI at SCG
Soyuz T-3 carries 3 cosmonauts to Salyut 6 space station, launched
Kepler Wessels scores century in 1st Test Cricket (162 v England)
Yasuhiro Nakasone elected Prime Minister of Japan succeeding Zenko Suzuki
Colombian jetliner Boeing 747 crashes in Madrid killing 181
Desmond Haynes out handled the ball v India
CardsVince Colemanwins NL Rookie of Year
Republic of Ireland gains consultative role in Northern Ireland
Europa TV, a project of five European public service broadcasters ceases operations after exhausting its budget
Young man survives 7 attempts at suicide in Somerset, England
Colombian jetliner bombed killing 107
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
George Harrisonreleases music singles "Cheer Down" and "Poor Little Girl"
US 63rd manned space mission STS 33 (Discovery 9) returns from space
"Howard Stern Interview" television program premieres on E! Network
Part of Vienna Hofburg destroyed by fire
India defeats West Indies in Bengal Jubilee Cricket Final, Kumble 6-12
Fire in disco in Fuxin, North-China, 233 killed
Julio Maria Sanguinetti elected president of Uruguay
25 people are killed in the second Souhane massacre in Algeria by suspected Islamist groups
Lions' Barry Sanders becomes NFL's second all-time rusher
Norway opens the world's longest road tunnel - the Laerdal tunnel linking Oslo and Bergen at 15.3-miles (24.6-kilometers)
Hydrogen atmosphere is discovered on the extrasolar planet Osiris by the Hubble Space Telescope, the first atmosphere detected on an extrasolar planet

Marco Antonio Barrera beats Erik Morales by majority decision to win WBC super featherweight boxing title at the MGM Grand Garden Arena, Las Vegas; final fight of legendary trilogy; The Ring's 'Fight of the Year'
New Zealand's All Blacks thump Six Nations Rugby Union Champions France 45-6 in Paris
First partial human face transplant is completed for Isabelle Dinoire in Amiens, France.
President El Hadj Omar Bongo Ondimba of Gabon, in power since 1967 and the longest-serving head of state in the world, is re-elected to his third consecutive seven-year term
Francesco Cossiga, Italian politician and former President of the Italian Republic, resigns from his position as lifetime senator
29 people are killed and 126 are wounded by 8 car bombings across Iraq
The Eurozone announces that it will make loans of 43.7 billion euros to Greece
Greece becomes the first developed market to be demoted into an emerging market by the MSCI index
Cricketer Phillip Hughes dies two days after being struck on the head by a bouncer
Stephanie Gilmore wins her 6th women's world surfing title
"Holy grail" of shipwrecks the San Jose, sunk 1708, is confirmed found by an international team off the coast of Colombia
Robert Lewis Dear (57) shots 3 dead and wounds 9 at a Planned Parenthood clinic in Colorado Springs, Colorado
8 Donkeys freed from jail after 4 days in Orai, Uttar Pradesh, India for eating plants
Bangkok Fire and Rescue Department confirm it has had 31,801 call outs to deal with snakes this year
Freight trains kills more than 100 reindeer over three days in Norway
North Korean ghost ship washes up Akita prefecture beach, Japan with 8 skeletons on board, 4th boat in a month
Convicted US murderer Samuel Little confirmed connected to 90 more murders of women after confessing details
Explosion near chemical company in Hebei Province, Northern China kills at least 23, injuring 22
Ghana celebrates the "year of return" marking 300 years since 1st African slave sold in America, by granting 125 people citizenship in special ceremony
Iran's most senior nuclear scientist Mohsen Fakhrizadeh assassinated outside Tehran, escalating tensions in the region
Mauna Loa, the world's largest active volcano, erupts for he first time in nearly 40 years on Hawaii island
A rebel coalition launches a surprise offensive in Syria, reaching into Aleppo for the first time since 2016
Former UK foreign secretary and Conservative Party leader William Hague is elected Chancellor of Oxford University
Large protests erupt in Georgia after the ruling party says it is suspending European Union accession until 2028
Truce agreed between Israel and Hezbollah brokered by the US and France with Lebanon saying 3,768 people have been killed in 13 months
Roman EmperorMarcus Aureliusgrants his sonCommodusaged 15 the rank of Imperator
English KingEdward Icalls what later becomes known as "The Model Parliament," extending the authority of its representatives

Christopher Columbusreturns to La Navidad colony, finding it destroyed by the first Native American uprising against Spanish rule. Taíno Cacique Caonabo led his people to attack the settlement after the brutal treatment they received from the garrison who disobeyed Columbus's orders.
Scottish kingJames IVreceives Perkin Warbeck, pretender to the English trone
John Walkerinvents friction matches in England
US Civil War:General Sherman's Union Army departs Sandersville, Georgia, burning its courthouse on his March to the Sea
Battle at Washita River, Oklahoma. GeneralGeorge A. Custerattacks group of Native American Indians, their chief Black Kettle dies in the attack
Swedish chemistAlfred Nobel's will establishes the Nobel Prize
"Also Sprach Zarathustra" (Thus Spake Zarathustra) byRichard Strauss, inspired byFriedrich Nietzsche's philosophical novel, debuts in Frankfurt
Gerhart Hauptmann's play "Der rote Hahn" premieres in Berlin
Ermanno Wolf-Ferrari's opera "Die Neugierigen Frauen" premieres in Munich, Germany
Compopser and statesmanIgnacy Jan Paderewskiresigns as Polish Prime Minister after ten months
First American superhero film, "The Mark of Zorro," directed by Fred Niblo and starringDouglas Fairbanks, is released in New York
Béla Bartók's ballet "Miraculous Mandarin" premieres at the Cologne Opera, Germany, conducted by Eugen Szenkar
Bank robberBaby Face Nelsonand two FBI agents die in a shoot-out in Barrington, Illinois
Bergen County (New Jersey) sheriff's department arrests American singerFrank Sinatra(23) on charge of "seduction", later withdrawn and amended to adultery, and shortly thereafter case dismissed
Maxwell Anderson's play "Key Largo" premieres in NYC
6th Heisman Trophy Award:Tom Harmon, Michigan (HB)
New York YankeeJoe DiMaggiois named AL MVP, for the 2nd time
Titoappoints Anti-fascist Liberation board in Yugoslavia
Dutch resistance fighter Hannie Schaft re-buried in presence ofQueen Wilhelmina
Peter Fraser's Labour government wins a second term in New Zealand's general elections
Al Oerterwins first of 4 consecutive men's discus gold medals when he throws Olympic record 56.36m to lead an American medal sweep at the Melbourne Games; Fortune Gordien silver, Desmond Koch bronze
Bobby Morrowtakes out the Melbourne Olympics sprint double when he wins the 200m gold medal in equal world record 20.75s; beats American teammates Andy Stanfield and Thane Baker
Félix Houphouët-Boignyelected unopposed as the 1st President of Ivory Coast (rules until 1993)
Gordie Howebecomes 1st NHLer to score 1,000 points
Patrice Lumumbaflees Leopoldville, Congo

Filming starts for "Star Trek" pilot "The Cage"; cast includes Jeffrey Hunter, Susan Oliver,Leonard Nimoy, and Majel Barret; although it never airs, some footage re-used in "Menagerie" episode of the series
French PresidentCharles de Gaullesaid 'Non!' to British entry to the European Common Market for the second time
Jimi Hendrixheadlines bill, supporting acts include The Move andPink Floyd, for 2 shows at Whitla Hall, Queens College, in Belfast - his only concerts in Ireland
Apple Records releasesGeorge Harrison's triple album set "All Things Must Pass" - it becomes the best selling solo release of any of the Beatles, going 6X Platinum
PopePaul VIwounded in chest during a visit to Philippines by a dagger-wielding Bolivian painter disguised as a priest
Neil Simon's play "Good Doctor" premieres in NYC
US PresidentRichard Nixonsigns the Emergency Petroleum Allocation Act, authorizing petroleum price, production, allocation and marketing controls
US Senate votes 92-3 to confirmGerald Fordas Vice President
San Francisco Mayor George Moscone and SupervisorHarvey Milkassassinated by former Supervisor Dan White at City Hall
Cuban poet and jornalist Maria Elene Cruz Varela sentenced to 2 years in prison for calling for the  democratization ofFidel Castro's regime
New Zealand's Labour Party defeats the National-led government after 9 years in power,Helen Clarkbecomes the first elected female Prime Minister in New Zealand's history
PopeJohn Paul IIreturns the relics of Saint John Chrysostom to the Eastern Orthodox Church.
Ballon d'Or: Italy defender Fabio Cannavaro (Juventus/Real Madrid) is named best football player in Europe ahead of Juventus goalkeeper Gianluigi Buffon and Arsenal forwardThierry Henry
Canadian House of Commons endorses Prime MinisterStephen Harper's motion to declare Québécois a nation within a unified Canada

German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelfinishes 2nd in season-ending Brazilian Grand Prix at Autódromo José Carlos Pace to retain his F1 World Drivers Championship; wins by 122 points from Jenson Button
Roger Federerclaims a record 6th ATP World Tour Finals tennis title with a 6–3, 6–7, 6–3 win over Frenchman Jo-Wilfried Tsonga in the final in London, England
"Frozen", the highest-grossing animated film of all time, starringIdina MenzelandKristen Bell, is released
German Mercedes driver Nico Rosberg clinches his only F1 World Driver's Championship with victory in season ending Abu Dhabi Grand Prix; wins title by 5 points from teammateLewis Hamilton
Great Britain'sPrince Harryand American actressMeghan Markleannounce their engagement
Pope Francisbegins a three-day trip to Myanmar, amid the Rohingya refugee crisis
US accuses Nicaragua's Vice-President Rosario Murillo, wife of PresidentDaniel Ortegaof human rights abuses and imposes sanctions
US PresidentDonald Trumpsigns two bills backing Hong Kong protesters, which check the territories autonomy and ban the sale of munitions to Hong Kong police, angering China
Albanian President Bajram Begaj grants citizenship to pop starDua Lipafor promoting the country through her international fame and her musical talents
Portuguese Royal Family and its court of nearly 15,000 people leave Lisbon for their colony of Brazil to escape invading Napoleonic troops
US Army withdraws from Little Rock, Arkansas after Central High School integration
